edges.target: Defective B-cell maturation and humoral immunity

edges.description: Defective B-cell class switching and maturation reduce IgG production.
edges.source: Defective B-cell maturation and humoral immunity

edges.description: Humoral immune deficiency predisposes to recurrent sinopulmonary infection.
edges.source: Defective B-cell maturation and humoral immunity

edges.description: Impaired antiviral T-cell control directly produces recurrent or persistent herpesvirus disease.
Defective antiviral control and EBV persistence contribute to APDS-associated lymphoma risk.
Loss of immune tolerance produces immune-mediated platelet destruction.
Loss of immune tolerance produces autoimmune erythrocyte destruction.
Persistent benign lymphoid hyperplasia presents clinically as lymph node enlargement.
Persistent benign lymphoid hyperplasia also produces splenic enlargement.
Chronic airway injury and remodeling produce established bronchiectasis.

nodes.description: Long-term prophylactic antibiotics are used in patients with recurrent bacterial sinopulmonary infections to reduce infectious burden alongside immunoglobulin replacement therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical spectrum and features of activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ syndrome: A large patient cohort study.
evidence.snippet: The majority of patients received immunoglobulin replacement and antibiotic prophylaxis, and 5 patients underwent hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.

nodes.description: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia is a recurrent autoimmune cytopenia in APDS and reflects breakdown of peripheral immune tolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders Related to PI3Kδ Hyperactivation: Characterizing the Clinical and Immunological Features of Activated PI3-Kinase Delta Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Cytopenias are the most common autoimmune manifestation (accounting for 76% of all autoimmune complications) and include immune thrombocytopenia purpura (ITP) and autoimmune haemolytic anaemia (AIHA) (3, 15, 19).

nodes.description: Immune thrombocytopenia is a characteristic autoimmune hematologic complication of APDS and reflects loss of immune tolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders Related to PI3Kδ Hyperactivation: Characterizing the Clinical and Immunological Features of Activated PI3-Kinase Delta Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Cytopenias are the most common autoimmune manifestation (accounting for 76% of all autoimmune complications) and include immune thrombocytopenia purpura (ITP) and autoimmune haemolytic anaemia (AIHA) (3, 15, 19).

nodes.description: Progressive airway remodeling with bronchiectasis is a major long-term consequence of recurrent infection and chronic inflammatory lung injury.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical spectrum and features of activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ syndrome: A large patient cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Thoracic imaging revealed high rates of mosaic attenuation (90%) and bronchiectasis (60%).

nodes.description: Benign lymphoid hyperplasia is a dominant APDS manifestation and presents as persistent lymphadenopathy, splenomegaly, tonsillar or adenoidal enlargement, and nodal disease at sites of chronic infection.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical spectrum and features of activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ syndrome: A large patient cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Recurrent sinopulmonary infections (98%) and nonneoplastic lymphoproliferation (75%) were common, often from childhood.

nodes.description: Reduced serum IgG is common and accompanies broader antibody production defects and poor vaccine responses.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical spectrum and features of activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ syndrome: A large patient cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Increased IgM levels (78%), IgG deficiency (43%), and CD4 lymphopenia (84%) were significant immunologic features.

nodes.description: Hyperactive PI3K-delta signaling skews B-cell development toward expanded transitional cells while impairing maturation into class-switched memory B cells and efficient IgG production. Clinically this produces a hyper-IgM pattern in many patients together with reduced antibody quality and poor polysaccharide vaccine responses.
nodes.id: Defective B-cell maturation and humoral immunity

evidence.reference_title: Enhanced AKT Phosphorylation of Circulating B Cells in Patients With Activated PI3Kδ Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Activated PI3Kδ syndrome (APDS) is a primary immunodeficiency characterized by recurrent respiratory tract infections, lymphoproliferation, and defective IgG production.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Genetic Features in Patients with Activated PI3Kδ Syndrome (APDS): a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: The predominant immunologic phenotype was hyper-IgM syndrome (48.1%). Immunologic profiling showed decreased B cells in 74.8% and CD4+ T cells in 64.8% of APDS patients.

nodes.description: Impaired antiviral control predisposes many patients to persistent or recurrent herpesvirus disease, especially EBV and CMV viremia or lymphadenitis.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical spectrum and features of activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ syndrome: A large patient cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Other significant complications included herpesvirus infections (49%), autoinflammatory disease (34%), and lymphoma (13%).

nodes.description: PI3K-delta hyperactivation also disrupts immune tolerance, producing autoimmune and autoinflammatory complications. Autoimmune cytopenias are the dominant hematologic autoimmune manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders Related to PI3Kδ Hyperactivation: Characterizing the Clinical and Immunological Features of Activated PI3-Kinase Delta Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Cytopenias are the most common autoimmune manifestation (accounting for 76% of all autoimmune complications) and include immune thrombocytopenia purpura (ITP) and autoimmune haemolytic anaemia (AIHA) (3, 15, 19).

nodes.description: Intravenous or subcutaneous immunoglobulin replacement is standard supportive therapy for antibody deficiency and reduces infectious burden.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Genetic Features in Patients with Activated PI3Kδ Syndrome (APDS): a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: The majority of APDS patients were placed on long-term immunoglobulin replacement therapy.

nodes.description: Dysfunctional T-cell immunity weakens control of latent herpesviruses, especially EBV and CMV, predisposing to persistent viremia, lymphadenitis, and lymphoma.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders Related to PI3Kδ Hyperactivation: Characterizing the Clinical and Immunological Features of Activated PI3-Kinase Delta Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: The immunodeficiency phenotype in APDS can be predominantly antibody deficiency, with recurrent sinopulmonary tract infections, or combined immunodeficiency, with a predisposition to herpesvirus in addition to bacterial infections.

nodes.description: Selective oral PI3Kdelta inhibition directly targets the causal signaling lesion in APDS and improves lymphadenopathy, abnormal B-cell subsets, serum IgM, and broader immune dysregulation.

evidence.reference_title: Effective "activated PI3Kδ syndrome"-targeted therapy with the PI3Kδ inhibitor leniolisib.
evidence.snippet: After 12 weeks of treatment, all patients showed amelioration of lymphoproliferation with lymph node sizes and spleen volumes reduced by 39% (mean; range, 26%-57%) and 40% (mean; range, 13%-65%), respectively.

evidence.reference_title: A randomised, placebo-controlled, phase III trial of leniolisib in activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase delta (PI3Kδ) syndrome (APDS): Adolescent and adult subgroup analysis.
evidence.snippet: In a 12-week phase III randomised placebo-controlled trial, leniolisib, a selective PI3Kδ inhibitor, was well-tolerated and met both co-primary endpoints (change from Baseline in log10-transformed sum of product of diameters of index lymph nodes and percentage of naïve/total B cells at Day 85).

nodes.description: Persistent or recurrent lymph node enlargement is part of the dominant benign lymphoproliferative phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders Related to PI3Kδ Hyperactivation: Characterizing the Clinical and Immunological Features of Activated PI3-Kinase Delta Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Lymphadenopathy occurs most frequently (61%), followed by splenomegaly (47%) and hepatomegaly (29%) (15).

nodes.description: B-cell lymphoma is the dominant malignancy complication in APDS and is often associated with chronic EBV infection.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical spectrum and features of activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ syndrome: A large patient cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Other significant complications included herpesvirus infections (49%), autoinflammatory disease (34%), and lymphoma (13%).

nodes.description: Germline activating variants in PIK3CD or PIK3R1 create constitutive PI3K-delta signaling with increased downstream AKT and mTOR activity. Persistent pathway activation disrupts lymphocyte homeostasis, promotes activation-induced death and senescence programs, and underlies the precision-medicine rationale for PI3K-delta inhibition.

biological_processes: activation-induced cell death of T cells

evidence.reference_title: Enhanced AKT Phosphorylation of Circulating B Cells in Patients With Activated PI3Kδ Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CD19+ B cells of peripheral blood in APDS2 patients showed the enhanced phosphorylation of AKT at Ser473 (pAKT) without any specific stimulation.

evidence.reference_title: Effective "activated PI3Kδ syndrome"-targeted therapy with the PI3Kδ inhibitor leniolisib.
evidence.snippet: Treatment with leniolisib (CDZ173), a selective PI3Kδ inhibitor, caused dose-dependent suppression of PI3Kδ pathway hyperactivation (measured as phosphorylation of AKT/S6) in cell lines ectopically expressing APDS-causative p110δ variants and in T-cell blasts derived from patients.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Genetic Features in Patients with Activated PI3Kδ Syndrome (APDS): a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: Activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase delta syndrome (APDS) is a novel primary immunodeficiency (PID) caused by heterozygous gain of function mutations in PI3Kδ catalytic p110δ (PIK3CD) or regulatory p85α (PIK3R1) subunits leading to APDS1 and APDS2, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Genetic Features in Patients with Activated PI3Kδ Syndrome (APDS): a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: The c.3061 G>A (p. E1021K) mutation in APDS1 with 85% frequency and c.1425+1 G> (A, C, T) (p.434-475del) mutation in APDS2 with 79% frequency were hotspot mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Genetic Features in Patients with Activated PI3Kδ Syndrome (APDS): a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: Activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase delta syndrome (APDS) is a novel primary immunodeficiency (PID) caused by heterozygous gain of function mutations in PI3Kδ catalytic p110δ (PIK3CD) or regulatory p85α (PIK3R1) subunits leading to APDS1 and APDS2, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Genetic Features in Patients with Activated PI3Kδ Syndrome (APDS): a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: The c.3061 G>A (p. E1021K) mutation in APDS1 with 85% frequency and c.1425+1 G> (A, C, T) (p.434-475del) mutation in APDS2 with 79% frequency were hotspot mutations.

nodes.description: Recurrent sinopulmonary infection together with airway obstruction from lymphoid hyperplasia promotes mosaic attenuation, bronchiolitis, and established bronchiectasis.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical spectrum and features of activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ syndrome: A large patient cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Thoracic imaging revealed high rates of mosaic attenuation (90%) and bronchiectasis (60%).

nodes.description: Recurrent upper and lower sinopulmonary infections are the dominant early clinical manifestation and often begin in infancy or early childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical spectrum and features of activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ syndrome: A large patient cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Recurrent sinopulmonary infections (98%) and nonneoplastic lymphoproliferation (75%) were common, often from childhood.

nodes.description: APDS promotes accumulation of senescent effector T cells and depletion of naive and long-lived memory T-cell pools, creating an exhausted effector-skewed T-cell compartment.

evidence.reference_title: Effective "activated PI3Kδ syndrome"-targeted therapy with the PI3Kδ inhibitor leniolisib.
evidence.snippet: Pathogenic gain-of-function variants in the genes encoding phosphoinositide 3-kinase δ (PI3Kδ) lead to accumulation of transitional B cells and senescent T cells, lymphadenopathy, and immune deficiency (activated PI3Kδ syndrome [APDS]).

nodes.description: mTOR inhibition with sirolimus is used as pathway-directed therapy for benign lymphoproliferation and selected immune-dysregulation manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Modulating the PI3K Signalling Pathway in Activated PI3K Delta Syndrome: a Clinical Perspective.
evidence.snippet: The mTOR inhibitor sirolimus has been used effectively for some clinical manifestations of this condition, however the arrival of specific PI3Kδ inhibitor leniolisib has shown promising early results and may provide a more targeted approach.

nodes.description: Splenic enlargement is a common manifestation of benign lymphoid hyperplasia in APDS and tracks with the broader lymphoproliferative phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders Related to PI3Kδ Hyperactivation: Characterizing the Clinical and Immunological Features of Activated PI3-Kinase Delta Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Lymphadenopathy occurs most frequently (61%), followed by splenomegaly (47%) and hepatomegaly (29%) (15).
